Class PauseScreen

java.lang.Object
game.Screens.PauseScreen
All Implemented Interfaces:
Screen
public class PauseScreen extends Object implements Screen
Displays a frozen BattleScreen. No entities move or change while this screen is active. Displays paused title, controls list, and current game timescale. Dev-mode controls remain active and are handled by ShadowAliens.

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    PauseScreen(Properties gameProps, BattleScreen battleScreen, int timeScale, double actualSpeed)
    Creates a new PauseScreen overlaying the given BattleScreen.

    Modifier and Type
    Method
    Description
    void
    draw()
    Draws the frozen BattleScreen underneath, then renders the pause overlay
    BattleScreen
    getBattleScreen()
    Returns the underlying BattleScreen so ShadowAliens can restore it as the current screen when player unpauses.
    void
    setTimeScale(int timeScale, double actualSpeed)
    Updates the displayed timescale and speed, called by ShadowAliens whenever G or F is pressed while paused.
    void
    update(bagel.Input input)
    No entity updates occur while paused as time is frozen.

  • Constructor Details

    • PauseScreen

      public PauseScreen(Properties gameProps, BattleScreen battleScreen, int timeScale, double actualSpeed)
      Creates a new PauseScreen overlaying the given BattleScreen.
      Parameters:
      gameProps - the loaded game properties file
      battleScreen - the active BattleScreen to draw frozen underneath
      timeScale - the current integer timescale
      actualSpeed - current speed multiplier

  • Method Details

    • update

      public void update(bagel.Input input)
      No entity updates occur while paused as time is frozen. Dev-mode controls are handled by ShadowAliens, not here.
      Specified by:
      update in interface Screen
      Parameters:
      input - the current frame's input

    • draw

      public void draw()
      Draws the frozen BattleScreen underneath, then renders the pause overlay
      Specified by:
      draw in interface Screen

    • setTimeScale

      public void setTimeScale(int timeScale, double actualSpeed)
      Updates the displayed timescale and speed, called by ShadowAliens whenever G or F is pressed while paused.
      Parameters:
      timeScale - the new integer timescale
      actualSpeed - the new fractional speed multiplier

    • getBattleScreen

      public BattleScreen getBattleScreen()
      Returns the underlying BattleScreen so ShadowAliens can restore it as the current screen when player unpauses.
      Returns:
      the BattleScreen this PauseScreen was created from
